key_points: Tribes are small communities sharing language, culture, and traditions
Nomads move from place to place searching for food, water, or pastures
Settled communities develop agriculture and permanent villages
Daily life in early communities involved hunting, gathering, farming, and animal rearing
Trade and migration helped spread ideas, culture, and technology
examples: Gond and Santhal tribes live in forests and follow traditional customs
Gujjar community moves with cattle in search of pastures
Early farming communities along the Ganga and Indus rivers settled to grow crops
Festivals and folk dances of tribes strengthen community bonds
Barter trade between farmers and nomads exchanged grains for milk or animal products
formulas: Tribe → Small community with shared culture and traditions
exam_tips: Remember the differences between tribes, nomads, and settled communities
Focus on how daily life and occupations were influenced by environment
Learn examples of festivals, art, and trade to illustrate culture
Understand how migration and settlement led to the evolution of communities
